meaning: n.<美口>行动迟缓的人,迟钝的人
examples: Now passengers are looking for a way to move like a 18 slowpoke.
I know he is a slowpoke, I wonder why he went to the airport so early this time!
That means big birds have to evolve different molting patterns to deal with their slowpoke feathers .
They calculated the birdy bandwidth by dividing data capacity by the length of the last pigeon's flight--a slowpoke at 3 hours, 51 minutes.
He even tried the technique on dinosaurs, and came to the conclusion that Tyrannosaurus Rex--the favorite extinct monster of schoolboys everywhere--was a relative slowpoke.
